---
title: "JobAgeProject"
author: "Obydah Jazaeri"
date: "2026-02-21"
output: html_document
runtime: shiny
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

library(janitor)    
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr) 
library(ggplot2)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
data2017 <- read_excel("cpsaat11b.xlsx")
data2018 <- read_excel("data2018.xlsx")
data2019 <- read_excel("data2019.xlsx")
```

```{r cleaning}
clean_cps_year <- function(df, year) {
  
  df %>%
    slice(-c(1:3)) %>%   # remove header junk
    rename(
      occupation = 1,
      median_age = 2,
      `16_to_19_years` = 3,
      `20_to_24_years` = 4,
      `25_to_34_years` = 5,
      `35_to_44_years` = 6,
      `45_to_54_years` = 7,
      `55_to_64_years` = 8,
      `65_years_and_over` = 9
    ) %>%
    filter(occupation != "Total employed") %>%
    
    # DROP median age before pivoting
    select(-median_age) %>%
    
    pivot_longer(
      cols = -occupation,
      names_to = "age_group",
      values_to = "employment_thousands"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      year = year,
      employment_thousands = readr::parse_number(employment_thousands)
    )
}

```


```{r draft graph}
cps_clean %>%
  filter(year == 2018) %>%
  group_by(occupation) %>%
  summarise(
    total_employment = sum(employment_thousands, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # keep only top 8 occupations so the pie is readable
  slice_max(total_employment, n = 8) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "", y = total_employment, fill = occupation)) +
  geom_col(width = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  labs(
    title = "Job Distribution by Occupation (2019)",
    fill = "Occupation"
  ) +
  theme_void()
```
